rulings.Case Title: WIRELESS SOLUTIONS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. STONESTOWN SHOPPING CENTER, L.P., A DELAWARE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO (1) DEEM THIS CASE IS RELATED TO A PENDING UNLAWFUL DETAINER CASE, CASE NO. CUD-22-669241; (2) TO CONSOLIDATE THE TWO CASES; AND (3) FOR A TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER STAYING PROCEEDINGS IN THE UD CASE PENDING DETERMINATION ON CONSOLIDATION OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, PENDING RESOLUTION OF THIS CASEF
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 29, 2022 line 13. MOTION TO (1) DEEM THIS CASE IS RELATED TO A PENDING UNLAWFUL DETAINER CASE, CASE NO. CUD-22-669241; (2) TO CONSOLIDATE THE TWO CASES; AND (3) FOR A TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER STAYING PROCEEDINGS IN THE UD CASE PENDING DETERMINATION ON CONSOLIDATION OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, PENDING RESOLUTION OF THIS CASEF is DENIED. Unlawful detainer is a summary proceeding and the moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that the circumstances of this case give rise to any known exception warranting consolidation. The Court further notes that the issue of validity of the termination clause is raised as an affirmative defense in the unlawful detainer case providing a remedy at law regarding such clause. The propriety of the notice of termination is at the heart of unlawful detainer action for possession and is addressed therein. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BOBB RETUTA VS. EDITHA BONIFACIO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 29, 2022 line 9. DEFENDANT EDITHA BONIFACIO, EDGARDO LACAP DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend Notice is fatally defective. CCP 1161 requires certain information to be included in the Notice if payment "may be made personally." Nothing on the face of the Notice suggests that in-person payment is not available i.e. that payment may not be made personally. If Plaintiff is only willing to accept payment by mail (as suggested in opposition (4:7-11) the notice should state: "payments must be mailed" not "payments may be mailed." The Court further notes that payment information , such as it is, is difficult to locate in the Notice, being included in the middle of a page filled with unrelated information and separated by ½ page of singe-spaced text from a heading titled "Payment Instructions." =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
